steps.title: Backsolve by testing answer choices
steps.content:
Substitute each choice's coordinates into both equations to find which point satisfies both.

Backsolving — testing choice C $(24, 16)$:
First equation: when $x = 24$, $y = -12(24) + 16 = -288 + 16 = -272 \neq 16$. Fails.

Fails. Testing choice A $(1, 4)$:
First equation: when $x = 1$, $y = -12(1) + 16 = -12 + 16 = 4$ ✓
Second equation: when $x = 1$, $y = -20(1) + 24 = -20 + 24 = 4$ ✓
The point $(1, 4)$ satisfies both equations. That matches choice A — A is correct.
